description_long: This is a military-style attack helicopter with a sleek, angular fuselage painted in a dark matte color, likely designed for stealth and combat operations. The helicopter features a prominent main rotor with four long, straight blades and a tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of a narrow tail boom. The cockpit is heavily armored with multiple angular, tinted windows providing visibility for the pilot and co-pilot. On either side of the fuselage, there are weapon pylons equipped with missile pods and other armaments, indicating its offensive capabilities. The landing gear consists of robust skids designed for stability on rough terrain. The overall design is aerodynamic and functional, with minimal visible markings and a focus on utility and survivability in urban or combat environments.
description: A dark, angular military attack helicopter with a four-blade main rotor, weapon pylons, and armored cockpit windows, designed for combat and urban operations.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the four-blade main rotor, narrow fuselage, and tail boom alignment.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cockpit windows, weapon pylons, and the full length of the tail boom with the tail rotor visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail rotor, landing skids, and the symmetrical arrangement of weapon pylons.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cockpit, weapon pylons, and tail rotor, with the helicopter's angular profile.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, colored black with no visible markings, each blade approximately 6-7 meters in length.
attribute_tail_rotor: Vertical tail rotor with four short blades, also black, mounted at the end of the tail boom with a protective fin structure.
attribute_landing_skids: Two robust, straight landing skids made of metal, painted dark, with crossbars for stability and no visible footpads.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple angular cockpit windows, likely four to six, arranged in a wraparound configuration, tinted dark with visible framing and no external markings.
